title: Savory Festive Croatian Padron Pepper Stew
description: A hearty, low‑sodium Croatian stew that celebrates the sweet, mild flavor of padron peppers with smoked paprika, tomato, and a hint of lemon zest.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika and stir to coat the onions and garlic.
Introduce the padron peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to blister, about 4–5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for another minute to deepen the flavor.
Pour in the vegetable broth, diced tomatoes, bay leaf, and thyme; bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, allowing the peppers to soften.
Remove the bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, then stir in lemon zest and chopped parsley.
Serve hot, spooned into bowls, and enjoy the aromatic, festive stew.
